description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a sloped array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a sturdy metal frame. Beneath the solar panels, there is a sheltered area housing various electrical components, including what appear to be inverters, battery storage units, and control boxes. The structure is set on a flat platform with marked zones and labels, suggesting organized access to different subsystems. The solar panels are arranged in a single, slightly tilted row, optimized for sunlight capture. The mounting frame is constructed from metallic posts and beams, providing stability and weather resistance. The overall design is modular and suitable for urban deployment, such as in parking lots, community centers, or public parks.
description: A modular solar energy installation with a sloped panel array on a metal frame, sheltering electrical and storage components below. The structure is compact, organized, and designed for urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sloped solar panel array and the sheltered electrical components beneath, with clear access to the main control area.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the linear arrangement of the panels and the distribution of electrical boxes and storage units on the platform.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar array and the organized layout of the electrical subsystems on the base platform.
description_view_3: Opposite side view revealing additional access points to the electrical components and a clear look at the supporting frame structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted, supported by a metal (likely aluminum or steel) frame with angled posts
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of 6 solar panels
